name: J-UNIWARD
summary: Adaptive JPEG steganography using a 3-level Daubechies-8 wavelet decomposition to assign per-DCT-coefficient embedding cost. Bits are placed in high-texture, high-noise regions using Syndrome-Trellis Codes, minimizing statistical detectability. The academic gold standard — all new JPEG steg schemes are benchmarked against it.
strength: Resists chi-square, SRM, and deep CNN steganalyzers (SRNet, Xu-Net) at low-to-medium payloads (< 0.3 bpnzac)
weaknesses: Detectable at high embedding rates (> 0.4 bpnzac); payload destroyed by re-compression at different JPEG quality
lineage: F5 (2001) → nsF5 (2007, shrinkage fix) → J-UNIWARD (2013, adaptive cost + STC)
